EXECUTION FOR MONEY ISSUED TO SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY AS TO DEFENDANT CASTILLO, JUVENAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE CLAIM OF EXEMPTION OF JUVENAL CASTILLO IS GRANTED IN PART. THE LEVYING OFFICER SHALL WITHHOLD $100 PER PAY PERIOD FROM JUVENAL CASTILLO FOR PAYMENT ON THE JUDGMENT. THE COURT FURTHER ORDERED THAT THE LEVYING OFFICER SHALL RELEASE 1/2 OF ANY FUNDS HELD TO THE DEFENDANT AND 1/2 TO THE JUDGMENT CREDITOR FOR PAYMENT ON THE JUDGMENT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR#6576, RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG.
ORDER DETERMINING CLAIM OF EXEMPTION (GRANTED IN PART)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
Off Calendar Sep-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Vivian L. Lerche, Esq., Tel. 415.680.5731, appearing specially for plaintiff and moving party MIDLAND FUNDING, LLC;
Juvenal Castillo, Tel. 650.308.5552, defendant/claimant appearing on his own behalf.

RULING: The Court examined defendant/claimant Juvenal Castillo. After hearing, the CLAIM OF EXEMPTION of Juvenal Castillo is granted in part. The levying officer shall withhold $100 per pay period from Juvenal Castillo for payment on the judgment. The Court further ordered that the levying officer shall release 1/2 of any funds held to the defendant and 1/2 to the judgment creditor for payment on the judgment. Order signed. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, rhunter@sftc.org.
